metadata.title: Build a Post-Build Learning Document Generator with Codex
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. The build experience turns into durable skill only if you capture what was learned immediately after — most builders skip that step.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. The build experience turns into durable skill only if you capture what was learned immediately after — most builders skip that step.

beats.narration_text: The post-build learning document is the Feynman test applied to your own build: if you can't write specifically what you learned and what you'd change, you borrowed the capability from Codex instead of building it yourself. The five-section format forces specificity — not 'learned about database design' but the exact Codex behavior discovered.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The post-build learning document is the Feynman test applied to your own build: if you can't write specifically what you learned and what you'd change, you borr

beats.narration_text: claude "Generate the 'What I learned I didn't know before' section of a post-build learning document. Be specific: name the exact Codex behavior discovered, the language feature encountered, or the workflow pattern observed. Do not write general lessons — write specific behaviors with file paths, function names, or API names." < build_notes.md

props.command: claude "Generate the 'What I learned' section. Be specific: name exact Codex behaviors, language features, or workflow patterns. Name file paths, function names, API names." < build_notes.md

beats.narration_text: learning_doc_generator.py — prompts user through 5-section form interactively, Claude generates the 'What I learned' and 'What I would do differently' sections with specific cited content, outputs formatted markdown.

props.code:
# interactive five-section post-build learning doc generator
import learning_engine, markdown_writer

build_notes = open('build_notes.md').read()
learned = learning_engine.generate_learned(build_notes)
would_change = learning_engine.generate_changes(build_notes)

doc = markdown_writer.format(sections=[
    ('What I built', build_notes),
    ('What I learned', learned),
    ('What I would do differently', would_change),
])
open('learning_doc.md', 'w').write(doc)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: learning_doc_generator.py — prompts user through 5-section form interactively, Claude generates the 'What I learned' and 'What I would do differently' sections

beats.narration_text: Generated 'What I learned' section: 4 specific entries. (1) Codex's default schema biases toward auto-increment integer keys — override with content-hash for idempotency. (2) AnimationPlayer must be a direct child of the node it animates — placing it as a sibling silently fails. (3) packed scene.instantiate() returns a copy. (4) delta accumulation needed for frame-independent motion. All four name exact behaviors.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Generated 'What I learned' section: 4 specific entries. (1) Codex's default schema biases toward auto-increment integer keys — override with content-hash for id

beats.narration_text: Extract 'What I would do differently' section; use it to generate a new AGENTS.md lessons-learned entry.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Extract 'What I would do differently' section; use it to generate a new AGENTS.md lessons-learned entry.

beats.narration_text: 'What I would do differently': 'write user needs with explicit viewport and font-size constraints for any phone-readable output.' Auto-generated AGENTS.md lessons entry: phone-readable outputs — specify max column count of 4 and minimum font size of 16px in user needs before running Code Mode. The post-build document closed the loop: build, reflection, permanent project memory.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: 'What I would do differently': 'write user needs with explicit viewport and font-size constraints for any phone-readable output.' Auto-generated AGENTS.md lesso

beats.narration_text: The specificity requirement is the whole point. 'Learned about packed scenes' is not learnable — it's a category. 'packed scene.instantiate() returns a copy; mutations on the returned instance don't affect the packed resource' is learnable — it's a fact you can act on next time. Specificity is what separates a document you wrote from a document you can use.
